root_text.sn35.8:1.3: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako atītasmiṁ cakkhusmiṁ anapekkho hoti;
root_text.sn35.8:1.5: paccuppannassa cakkhussa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.sn35.8:1.10: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako atītāya jivhāya anapekkho hoti;
root_text.sn35.8:1.12: paccuppannāya jivhāya nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.sn35.8:1.16: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako atītasmiṁ manasmiṁ anapekkho hoti;
root_text.sn35.8:1.18: paccuppannassa manassa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.8:0.3: The Interior as Suffering in the Three Times
translation_text.sn35.8:1.1: “Mendicants, the eye of the past and future is suffering,
translation_text.sn35.8:1.3: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple is not concerned with the eye of the past,
translation_text.sn35.8:1.4: they don’t look forward to enjoying the eye in the future,
translation_text.sn35.8:1.5: and they practice for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding the eye in the present. …”

reference_text.sn35.8:1.1: csp1ed13.239, csp2ed13.239, dr18.5, ms13S4_17, msdiv8, ndp14.6, sya18.5
